---
title: "Reflection"
---

## What I Learned

Throughout this module I developed skills across several areas:

- **Git & Version Control** — Learning to commit incrementally and write 
  descriptive messages made tracking changes much easier.
- **Quarto** — Building a full website with Quarto taught me how to combine 
  code, narrative, and design in one place.
- **Python** — I strengthened my data analysis and visualisation skills using 
  pandas and Plotly.
- **Web Design** — I learned how SCSS, themes, and layout work together to 
  create a professional site.

## My Workflow

Here is how I approached building this portfolio:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Plan Structure] --> B[Set Up Quarto]
  B --> C[Build Pages]
  C --> D[Add Python Code]
  D --> E[Style with SCSS]
  E --> F[Deploy to GitHub Pages]
```

## Data Analytics in Practice

A key concept in analytics is the linear regression formula:

$$y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x + \epsilon$$

Where $y$ is the predicted outcome, $x$ is the input variable, 
and $\epsilon$ represents the error term.[^2]

## Challenges

::: {.callout-warning}
The most challenging part was understanding YAML indentation — small 
formatting errors can break the entire page.
:::

Write 1–2 more specific challenges you faced and how you solved them.

## A Useful Resource

Here is a short introduction to data analytics that I found helpful:

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yZvFH7B6gKI >}}

## Connecting to My Future

::: {.callout-tip}
These skills are directly relevant to roles in data analytics, where 
communicating findings clearly is just as important as the analysis itself.
:::

Write how these skills connect to your future career goals.

[^2]: This formula forms the basis of predictive modelling, a core skill 
in business analytics.
